response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with 16+ years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project — far below the 8–12 years of hands-on C++ development the position demands. He has no demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IEC 62443 secure development practices, MQTT, OPC UA, or any of the industrial protocols listed. His primary expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web/cloud platforms, which only partially overlap with the advantageous skills section of the JD, not its core requirements.
strengths: Proven event-driven architecture and distributed-systems experience (GISCollective platform, Sociomantic micro-services at millions of req/s)
Node.js and React skills match the advantageous requirements; agile/TDD discipline is strong throughout career
Broad polyglot background and demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly across embedded, web, and mobile domains
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months, 2014–2015) and does not come close to the 8–12 year requirement for the core technology stack
Zero evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or any industrial-cybersecurity domain knowledge
Role requires a senior-level C++ architect and security practitioner; candidate's seniority is in web/TypeScript ecosystems, making a pivot here a high-risk hire
